AI Intelligence
● Breaking
ECHA's simultaneous addition of the fluorinated bisphenol and n-hexane to the SVHC Candidate List on 2026-02-04 is the most urgent 7-day signal — Article 33 communication obligations to downstream users and consumers are triggered immediately upon Candidate List inclusion, meaning supplier declarations and product data sheets referencing these substances are already non-compliant if not updated.
6-Month Trend
ECHA has maintained steady Candidate List expansion activity over the past 6 months, adding three new SVHCs spanning flame retardants, industrial solvents, and fluorinated compounds — all highly relevant to windows/doors manufacturing and supply chains. The dominant theme is convergence: ECHA is simultaneously targeting legacy halogenated flame retardants (DBDPE), common industrial solvents (n-hexane), and fluorinated bisphenols that intersect directly with PFAS regulatory pressure. Activity is concentrated in a short burst rather than spread across the period, suggesting ECHA batch-processing additions ahead of anticipated Authorization List updates.
Last 30 Days
No new regulatory filings or enforcement actions were captured in the 30-day window beyond the February SVHC additions already flagged as breaking news. The absence of additional developments suggests the compliance team has a brief window to act on the February Candidate List additions before downstream obligations (supplier notifications, SDS updates, Article 33 customer communication duties) become overdue. No ECHA restriction proposals or Authorization List amendments were published in this period.
Strategic Priorities
  • Immediately screen your Assent platform supplier declarations and internal BOM/formulation data against all three new SVHCs (fluorinated bisphenol, DBDPE, n-hexane) — Article 33 obligations are already active and a 45-day clock for customer communication is effectively running.
  • Issue a targeted supplier RFI through Assent specifically flagging the fluorinated bisphenol SVHC addition to all coating, adhesive, and sealant suppliers, cross-referencing your existing PFAS supplier inquiry to consolidate fluorinated substance risk into a single supplier response track.
  • Update your product Safety Data Sheets and Article 33 communication templates for any EU-distributed product lines where DBDPE presence in profiles or gaskets is unconfirmed — treat absence of data as presumptive presence until suppliers respond, to protect against downstream customer claims.
LOW IMPACT 3 articles / 6mo
Company Impact
Products Bisphenol AF (SVHC as of Feb 4, 2026) is a known precursor monomer in fluoropolymer/fluoroelastomer chemistry — any fluoropolymer-coated components destined for EU distribution must be screened immediately for Bisphenol AF content above 0.1% w/w to assess Article 33 notification obligations. Supply Chain Purchased electronics, O-rings, gaskets, seals, adhesives, and cleaning agents are at risk across all three newly listed SVHCs — n-hexane (adhesives/cleaners), Bisphenol AF (fluoroelastomer seals), and DBDPE (electronics/PCBs) — requiring updated declarations of conformity from suppliers. Actions
  • Screen all fluoropolymer-coated components intended for EU distribution against Bisphenol AF (CAS 1478-61-1) and n-hexane (CAS 110-54-3) at the 0.1% w/w threshold; update product data sheets and Safety Data Sheets accordingly by end of Q1 2026.
  • Update the Assent platform SVHC substance tracking list with all three new additions (n-hexane, Bisphenol AF, DBDPE) and trigger re-declaration requests from suppliers of affected article categories.
Supplier
  • Issue written requests to all EU-based and global suppliers of electronics, seals, gaskets, adhesives, and cleaning agents demanding updated SVHC declarations of conformity reflecting the February 4, 2026 Candidate List additions within 45 days.
  • Specifically flag fluoroelastomer and fluoropolymer component suppliers to confirm Bisphenol AF content and whether they have issued Article 33 downstream notifications — escalate non-responders given the direct overlap with the company's PFAS supply chain.
Strategy Note
With Bisphenol AF now on the SVHC Candidate List as of February 4, 2026, the compliance team must act this week to assess whether this monomer is present in fluoropolymer coatings or purchased fluoroelastomer components above the 0.1% w/w threshold, given its direct overlap with the company's PFAS product lines and ongoing Assent declarations work. Simultaneously, the existing supplier outreach campaign should be updated to incorporate all three new SVHC additions, with targeted follow-up to electronics, seal, adhesive, and solvent suppliers whose products are likely candidates for n-hexane and DBDPE presence. Supplier responses on DBDPE (listed November 2025) should already be expected — chase any outstanding declarations before the February 2026 additions add further workload.
